natural_language_analysis.summary: Both return witnesses are semantically valid. Select the second disjunct in each generated return witness, preserve xizi_dllseg by identity, and establish only the branch-specific head-value equality by case analysis on nodes.

vc_shape.pre_spatial: xizi_dllseg first linklist_pre linklist_pre last nodes in the selected second disjunct
vc_shape.pre_pure: PreH1: first = linklist_pre; PreH2: first = xizi_dll_first_value linklist_pre nodes
vc_shape.pre_exists: For a nonempty nodes list, unfolding xizi_dllseg exposes its local next address existential after the pure cur=node and cur<>sentinel facts; the existential need not be instantiated to prove the contradiction.
vc_shape.post_spatial: The identical xizi_dllseg first linklist_pre linklist_pre last nodes resource

witnesses.witness_instantiation: No right-side existential is required. Destruct nodes. For nil, xizi_dll_head_value nil reduces to NULL, hence 0. For node :: rest, the xizi_dllseg pure prefix gives first <> linklist_pre while PreH1 gives first = linklist_pre, so the spatial antecedent is impossible.
witnesses.space_plan: Choose the second disjunct. Retain xizi_dllseg unchanged for the right conjunct. In the nil branch it unfolds to a pure closure condition conjoined with emp and can be reconstructed or cancelled; in the cons branch expose only the predicate's pure prefix to derive contradiction, without consuming or inventing resources.
witnesses.pure_plan: Destruct nodes and simplify xizi_dll_head_value and xizi_dllseg. The nil branch reduces the target to 0 = NULL. The cons branch uses the unfolded xizi_dllseg fact first <> linklist_pre together with PreH1 first = linklist_pre; ex falso closes the pure equality and the conjunction.
witnesses.refinement_plan: not applicable; this VC has no safeExec/refinement state

analysis_groups.shared_proof_pattern: Select the weaker second return disjunct, destruct nodes, reduce xizi_dll_first_value/xizi_dll_head_value, discharge an impossible branch from sentinel equality/inequality, and frame the unchanged xizi_dllseg.
analysis_groups.shared_space_plan: The xizi_dllseg antecedent is returned unchanged; only return_wit_1's impossible nonempty branch needs its pure prefix exposed.
analysis_groups.shared_pure_plan: Both proofs are list-case analyses using PreH1/PreH2 and definitional reduction; no arithmetic or extra premise is required.
analysis_groups.grouping_reason: The two witnesses are from the same function, share the exact abstract-list destructor proof pattern, require no separate helper family, and the combined size 2 is below the controller maximum 12.

proof_groups.proof_strategy: For each witness unfold the return-witness definition, select the second disjunct, introduce the parameters, destruct nodes, simplify xizi_dll_first_value/xizi_dll_head_value, and preserve xizi_dllseg by identity. In return_wit_1 unfold xizi_dllseg only in the impossible cons case to obtain first <> linklist_pre against PreH1. In return_wit_2 the nil case contradicts PreH1 through the simplified PreH2, while the cons case closes from PreH2.

per_witness_plan.proof_of_xizi_double_link_head_return_wi: right; intros; destruct nodes; nil closes by head-value reduction and spatial identity; cons closes by contradiction between PreH1 and the pure non-sentinel fact exposed from xizi_dllseg
right; intros; destruct nodes; nil contradicts PreH1 via simplified PreH2; cons obtains the head equality from simplified PreH2; cancel the unchanged xizi_dllseg

proof_groups.space_plan: Frame/cancel xizi_dllseg unchanged; expose its pure cons-case prefix only for return_wit_1 contradiction.
proof_groups.pure_plan: Destruct nodes and simplify the two mathematical list destructors; use exactly PreH1, PreH2, and return_wit_1's xizi_dllseg non-sentinel fact.

proof_groups.grouping_reason: Both witnesses share a single compact list-destructor/framing proof pattern and have no dependency or helper boundary; one group minimizes workers while respecting max_witnesses_per_group=12.
